title: 10 medicinal preparations of Cassia fistula (golden shower)

preparations_list.preparation_desc: Cassia fistula teas is used to alleviate various health issues. It has been traditionally employed to treat digestive problems such as constipation, diarrhea, and bloating due to its natural laxative properties. The tea is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it effective in reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues like bronchitis, and even treating skin conditions like eczema and acne.

Cassia fistula tinctures is used to treat a variety of health issues. The tincture is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, cuts, and skin infections. It is also used to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues such as diarrhea, and soothe respiratory problems like bronchitis. Additionally, the tincture has been known to help lower blood sugar levels and even show promise in treating certain types of cancer.

Cassia fistula decoctions is used to treat a range of health issues. The decoction has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of diarrhea, dysentery, and other gastrointestinal disorders. It is also employed to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Additionally, the decoction is believed to have antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for wounds, ulcers, and respiratory infections.

Cassia fistula capsules is used to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues. The capsule's active compounds, such as saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating respiratory infections, skin disorders, and digestive problems. Cassia fistula tablets is used to alleviate various health issues. It's also employed in traditional medicine to soothe conditions like eczema, acne, and dermatitis. Additionally, Cassia fistula ointment has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective against bacterial and fungal infections.

Cassia fistula creams is used to alleviate various skin issues such as eczema, acne, and wounds. The anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties of the cream help to reduce redness and swelling, promoting faster healing. It is also used to treat hair and scalp conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, and alopecia areata. Cassia fistula salves is used to treat a variety of health issues. The salve has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in soothing skin irritations, wounds, and burns. It's also used to reduce fever, relieve digestive problems, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections such as bronchitis and asthma. intro: Cassia fistula has several medicinal preparations, such as decoctions, teas, and tinctures. These preparations are useful for treating various health issues, including fever, dysentery, diarrhea, and skin conditions like eczema and ulcers. The plant's bark is used to make a tea that helps reduce inflammation and treats respiratory problems, while the seeds are used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and jaundice. Additionally, the plant's leaves are used to make a tincture that can be used topically to treat wounds and skin infections.
intro_desc: Cassia fistula has several medicinal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions. These preparations are traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems. The tea is useful for reducing inflammation, while the tincture is used to treat diarrhea and dysentery. The infusion is also used to treat eye infections and skin irritations.
